title: [lichen_l2.c:599] lichen_peer_add array decay - no runtime size enforcement
description:
Contract: lichen_peer_add(const uint8_t eui64[8], const uint8_t pubkey[32]) documents that eui64 MUST be 8 bytes and pubkey MUST be 32 bytes.
Violation: C arrays decay to pointers, providing no runtime bounds checking. The memcpy on lines 687-688 copies exactly LICHEN_EUI64_LEN (8) and LICHEN_L2_PUBKEY_LEN (32) bytes unconditionally. A caller passing a smaller buffer causes buffer overread.
Impact: Undefined behavior (memory access beyond buffer bounds) if caller passes undersized buffers. Defense relies entirely on caller discipline.
Mitigation: This is a language limitation. The existing BUILD_ASSERTs (lines 108-111) verify constant consistency but cannot validate caller buffer sizes. Document prominently that this is a SECURITY boundary that callers must respect.
close_reason: Added __attribute__((nonnull)) to lichen_peer_add() and lichen_peer_remove() for compile-time NULL checks. Added SECURITY comment to lichen_peer_remove() documenting the array size contract (matching existing documentation in lichen_peer_add). The array decay limitation is inherent to C; these changes add compiler-enforced NULL checks and make the caller contract explicit in both function docstrings.
